volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 2.3.448c - Section 2.4.490)

sections.label: Section 2.3.448c
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.449
sections.translation: I didn ’ t take her for a slave ; but it was he that took her for such .

sections.label: Section 2.3.449b
sections.translation: Let me alone .

sections.label: Section 2.3.450
sections.original: Non potes tu lege vendere illam .
sections.translation: By law you cannot put her up for sale .

sections.label: Section 2.3.450b
sections.translation: I ’ ll somehow see to that .

sections.label: Section 2.3.451
sections.original: Post autem communest illa mihi cum álio . qui scio
sections.translation: And then besides , she ’ s the common property of myself and another person ; how do I know how he ’ s disposed , whether he does wish or doesn ’ t wish to sell her ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.453
sections.translation: I ’ m sure he does wish .

sections.label: Section 2.3.453b
sections.original: At pol ego esse credo aliquem qui non velit .
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , I believe that there ’ s a certain person who doesn ’ t wish .

sections.label: Section 2.3.454
sections.translation: What matters that to me ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.454b
sections.original: Quia illi suam rem esse aequomst in manu .
sections.translation: Because it ’ s right that he should have the disposal of his own property .

sections.label: Section 2.3.455
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.455b
sections.original: Communis mihi illa est cum illo : is hic nunc non adest .
sections.translation: She is the common property of myself and another person ; he isn ’ t here at present .

sections.label: Section 2.3.456
sections.translation: You are answering me before I ask .

sections.label: Section 2.3.456b
sections.original: Prius tú emis quam vendo , pater .
sections.translation: You are buying , father , before I sell . I don ’ t know , I say , whether he chooses to part with her or not .

sections.label: Section 2.3.458
sections.original: Quid ? illi quoidam qui mandavit tibi si emetur , tum volet ,
sections.translation: But if she is purchased for that certain person who gave you the commission , will he choose it then ? If I purchase her for that person who gave me the commission , will he then not choose it ? You avail nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.3.460
sections.original: numquam edepol quisquam illam habebit potius quam ille quem ego volo .
sections.translation: Never , on my faith , shall any person have her in preference to the person that I wish . That I ’ m resolved upon .

sections.label: Section 2.3.461
sections.translation: Have you made up your mind that it is resolved upon ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.461b
sections.original: Censen certum esse ? quin ad navem iam hinc eo ,
sections.translation: Why , I ’ m going hence at once to the ship ; there she shall be sold .

sections.label: Section 2.3.462b
sections.translation: Do you wish me to go there with you ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.462c
sections.translation: I don ’ t wish you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.462d
sections.translation: You don ’ t choose it , then .

sections.label: Section 2.3.463
sections.original: Meliust te , quae sunt mandatae res tibi , praevortier .
sections.translation: It ’ s better for you to give your earliest attention to the business which you ’ ve been commissioned upon .

sections.label: Section 2.3.464
sections.translation: You are hindering me from doing so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.464b
sections.original: At me incusato : te fecisse sedulo .
sections.translation: Then do you make your excuse that you have used all diligence .

sections.label: Section 2.3.465
sections.original: ad portum ne bitas , dico iam tibi .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you go to the harbour , I tell you that now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.465b
sections.translation: That shall be attended to .

sections.label: Section 2.3.466
sections.original: Ibo ad portum . ne hic resciscat , cauto opust : non ipse emam ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll be off to the harbour , and ( I have need of caution lest he should find it out ) I ’ ll not buy her myself , but commission my friend Lysimachus ; he said just now that he was going to the harbour . I ’ m delaying while I ’ m standing here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.468b
sections.translation: I ’ m lost — I ’ m undone .

sections.label: Section 2.4.469
sections.original: Pentheum diripuisse aiunt Bacchas : nugas maximas
sections.translation: They say that the Bacchanals tore Pentheus to pieces [*] . I do believe that that was the merest trifle compared with the manner in which I am rent asunder in different ways .

sections.label: Section 2.4.471
sections.original: cur ego vivo ? cur non morior ? quid mihist in vita boni ?
sections.translation: Why do I exist ? Why don ’ t I die ? What good is there for me in life ? I ’ m determined , I ’ ll go to a doctor [*] , and there I ’ ll put myself to death by poison , since that is being taken from me for the sake of which I desire to remain in existence .

sections.label: Section 2.4.474
sections.translation: Stop , prithee , stop , Charinus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.474b
sections.translation: Who is it , that calls me back ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.474c
sections.translation: Eutychus ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.475
sections.original: tuos amicus et sodalis , simul vicinus proxumus .
sections.translation: your friend and companion , your nearest neighbour as well .

sections.label: Section 2.4.476
sections.original: Non tu scis , quantum malarum rerum sustineam .
sections.translation: You don ’ t know [*] what a vast weight of my woes I am enduring .

sections.label: Section 2.4.476b
sections.translation: I do know . I listened to it all at the door : I know the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 2.4.478
sections.translation: What is it that you know ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.478b
sections.translation: Your father wishes to sell —

sections.label: Section 2.4.478c
sections.translation: You have the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 2.4.479
sections.translation: — Your mistress —

sections.label: Section 2.4.479b
sections.translation: You know by far too much .

sections.label: Section 2.4.479c
sections.translation: — Against your wish .

sections.label: Section 2.4.480
sections.original: Plurimum tu scis . sed qui scis esse amicam illam meam ?
sections.translation: You know everything . But how do you know that this woman is my mistress ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.481
sections.translation: You yourself told me yesterday .

sections.label: Section 2.4.481b
sections.translation: Isn ’ t it the fact that I had quite forgotten that I told you yesterday ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.482b
sections.translation: It ’ s not surprising it is so .

sections.label: Section 2.4.482c
sections.translation: I now consult you . Answer me ; by what death do you think that I should die in preference ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.484
sections.original: Non taces ? cave tu istuc dixis .
sections.translation: Won ’ t you hold your peace ? Take you care how you say that .

sections.label: Section 2.4.484b
sections.translation: What then do you wish me to say ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.485
sections.original: Vin patri sublinere pulchre me os tuo ?
sections.translation: Should you like me to trick your father nicely ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.485b
sections.translation: I really should like it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.486
sections.translation: Should you like me to walk to the harbour —

sections.label: Section 2.4.486b
sections.translation: What , rather than that you should fly ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.486c
sections.translation: And release the fair one for a sum .

sections.label: Section 2.4.487b
sections.translation: What , rather than you should pay her weight in gold ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.487c
sections.translation: Whence is it to come ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.488
sections.original: Achillem orabo , aurum ut mihi det , Hector qui expensus fuit .
sections.translation: I ’ ll entreat Achilles to lend me the gold with which Hector was ransomed

sections.label: Section 2.4.489
sections.translation: Are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.489b
sections.original: Pol sanus si sim , non te medicum mi expetam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if I were in my senses , I shouldn ’ t be seeking you for my physician .

sections.label: Section 2.4.490
sections.original: Tanti quanti poscit , vin tanti illam emi ?
sections.translation: Do you wish her to be purchased for as high a price as he asks ?
